   318→== Core Principles
   319→
   320→The Axial Lexicon provides reusable, atomic terminology for technical specifications.
   321→Each term maintains precise semantics while adapting to implementation context.
   322→Specifications selectively import only needed axial terms, avoiding vocabulary bloat.
   323→
   324→== Motif Vocabulary
   325→
   326→AXL terms are
   327→{axl_motif_s}
   328→- abstract patterns that concrete specifications voice in their local context.
   329→
   330→[[axl_motif]]
   331→{axl_motif}::
   332→An abstract pattern defined in the Axial Lexicon that can be voiced by concrete terms in specifications.
   333→A
   334→{axl_motif}
   335→captures the essential shape of a concept while remaining independent of implementation context.
   336→Specifications voice
   337→{axl_motif_s}
   338→through local terms that bind the abstract pattern to specific environments.
   339→
   340→[[axl_voicing]]
   341→{axl_voicing}::
   342→A concrete term in a specification that gives local expression to A
   343→{axl_motif}.
   344→Each
   345→{axl_voicing}
   346→adapts the abstract pattern to a specific context while preserving the
   347→{axl_motif_s}
   348→essential character.
   349→
   350→[[axl_voices]]
   351→{axl_voices}::
   352→The relationship where a concrete term expresses a
   353→{axl_motif}
   354→in a specific context.
   355→When a term
   356→{axl_voices}
   357→a
   358→{axl_motif},
   359→it gives the abstract pattern local form and implementation-specific behavior.
   360→
   361→[[axl_evokes]]
   362→{axl_evokes}::
   363→The relationship where A
   364→{axl_motif}
   365→calls forth its
   366→{axl_voicing_s}
   367→in the reader's understanding.
   368→When encountering A
   369→{axl_motif},
   370→it
   371→{axl_evokes}
   372→the concrete realizations known from various specifications.
   373→
   374→[[axl_annotation]]
   375→{axl_annotation}::
   376→A
   377→{xref_MCM}
   378→comment using Strachey brackets that declares the
   379→{axl_motif_s}
   380→a term
   381→{axl_voices}.
   382→The
   383→{axl_annotation}
   384→appears between anchor and definition as a space-separated list of AXL terms within Strachey brackets.
   385→+
   386→Format: `// ⟦relationship primary [dimension ...]⟧`
   387→+
   388→* First term: the relationship (typically `axl_voices`)
   389→* Second term: the primary
   390→{axl_motif}
   391→being voiced
   392→* Remaining terms: dimension terms (prefixed `axd_`) specifying characteristics
   393→+
   394→Examples:
   395→+
   396→* `// ⟦axl_voices axc_call⟧` - simple voicing
   397→* `// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable⟧` - voicing with dimensions
   398→+
   399→Category prefixes carry semantic meaning, eliminating need for punctuation.
   400→Position determines role: relationship first, primary second, dimensions following.
   401→This design prioritizes LLM interpretability over human visual parsing.
   402→When ambiguity or friction arises during LLM processing, the format should evolve to resolve it.
   403→
   404→== Motif Implications
   405→
   406→{axl_motif_s}
   407→carry implied characteristics beyond their definitions.
   408→These implications guide how
   409→{axl_voicing_s}
   410→should behave:
   411→
   412→* Anchor placement expectations per
   413→{xref_MCM}
   414→form patterns
   415→* Type/instance level - where the
   416→{axl_motif}
   417→sits in abstraction hierarchy
   418→* Contextual requirements - parent structures or operations the
   419→{axl_motif}
   420→exists within
   421→* Voicing expectation - whether the
   422→{axl_motif}
   423→typically requires local voicing
   424→
   425→== Form Expectations
   426→
   427→Each AXL category implies an expected
   428→{xref_MCM}
   429→definition form pattern for documents using that category.
   430→During type-scheme linting, these expectations guide validation of definition structure.
   431→
   432→* {axc_*} control terms expect
   433→{xref_MCM}
   434→`mcm_form_deflist`
   435→* {axo_sequence} operations expect
   436→{xref_MCM}
   437→`mcm_form_section` with nested steps
   438→* {axj_structure} definitions expect
   439→{xref_MCM}
   440→`mcm_form_section`
   441→* {axj_field} definitions expect
   442→{xref_MCM}
   443→`mcm_form_bullet` under parent structure
   444→* {axk_premise} definitions expect
   445→{xref_MCM}
   446→`mcm_form_deflist`
   447→* {axs_*} section terms expect
   448→{xref_MCM}
   449→`mcm_form_section` for section headers
   450→* Most other categories expect
   451→{xref_MCM}
   452→`mcm_form_deflist`
   453→
   454→These expectations support the
   455→{axl_annotation}
   456→pattern where documents include `// ⟦axl_voices motif ...⟧` markers that are validated when AXL is in context.
   457→
   458→== Axial Control Terms
   459→
   460→[[axc_variable]]
   461→{axc_variable}::
   462→A
   463→{axl_motif}
   464→representing a temporary value holder denoted by distinctive delimiters that exists for the duration of an operational sequence.
   465→Implementation varies by context: shell variables in bash, dictionary entries in Python, sessionStorage in browser JavaScript.
   466→
   467→[[axc_fatal]]
   468→{axc_fatal}::
   469→A
   470→{axl_motif}
   471→representing immediate termination of the operational sequence with error status, preventing all subsequent steps from executing.
   472→Requires operator intervention or error recovery before retry.
   473→
   474→[[axc_warn]]
   475→{axc_warn}::
   476→A
   477→{axl_motif}
   478→representing display of a non-blocking warning message while allowing the operational sequence to continue execution.
   479→Alerts operators to concerning conditions without halting progress.
   480→
   481→[[axc_store]]
   482→{axc_store}::
   483→A
   484→{axl_motif}
   485→representing capture of a value from an operation result or computation into a
   486→{axc_variable}
   487→for later reference.
   488→Context determines storage mechanism while preserving semantic intent.
   489→
   490→[[axc_use]]
   491→{axc_use}::
   492→A
   493→{axl_motif}
   494→representing reference to a previously stored
   495→{axc_variable}
   496→value in the current operation.
   497→Retrieves stored values from context-appropriate storage location.
   498→
   499→[[axc_require]]
   500→{axc_require}::
   501→A
   502→{axl_motif}
   503→representing validation check that confirms an expected condition, triggering
   504→{axc_fatal}
   505→or alternate execution path if not met.
   506→Enforces preconditions and invariants throughout execution.
   507→
   508→[[axc_poll]]
   509→{axc_poll}::
   510→A
   511→{axl_motif}
   512→representing repeated execution of a status check at defined intervals until completion condition or timeout.
   513→Implements retry logic with configurable intervals and limits.
   514→
   515→[[axc_call]]
   516→{axc_call}::
   517→A
   518→{axl_motif}
   519→representing direct invocation of an external service, API endpoint, or command.
   520→Captures both success and error responses for processing.
   521→
   522→[[axc_submit]]
   523→{axc_submit}::
   524→A
   525→{axl_motif}
   526→representing initiation of an asynchronous operation that returns immediately with a handle for later completion tracking.
   527→Distinct from
   528→{axc_call}
   529→which expects synchronous response.
   530→
   531→[[axc_await]]
   532→{axc_await}::
   533→A
   534→{axl_motif}
   535→representing blocking wait on a submitted asynchronous operation until completion.
   536→Polls operation status until done, then returns final result or error.
   537→
   538→[[axc_show]]
   539→{axc_show}::
   540→A
   541→{axl_motif}
   542→representing presentation of operational facts or values to the operator.
   543→Implementation varies from console output to UI display based on context.
   544→
   545→[[axc_step]]
   546→{axc_step}::
   547→A
   548→{axl_motif}
   549→representing a normal progress message indicating successful completion of a processing stage.
   550→Provides operational visibility without implying warnings or errors.
   551→
   552→== Axial Environment Terms
   553→
   554→{axe_environment_s}
   555→specify execution contexts for control operations.
   556→{axl_voicing_s}
   557→of
   558→{axc_call},
   559→{axc_warn},
   560→{axc_fatal}
   561→and similar control motifs should include an appropriate
   562→{axe_environment}
   563→as a dimension to clarify how the control term manifests in that context.
   564→
   565→[[axe_environment]]
   566→{axe_environment}::
   567→A
   568→{axl_motif}
   569→representing an execution context that determines how control terms manifest.
   570→{axe_environment_s}
   571→appear as dimensions in
   572→{axl_voicing_s}
   573→to bind abstract control semantics to concrete operational behavior.
   574→
   575→[[axe_bash_interactive]]
   576→{axe_bash_interactive}::
   577→A
   578→{axl_motif}
   579→representing a bash shell session with human operator present at an interactive terminal.
   580→Feedback via stdout/stderr is immediate.
   581→Operator can interrupt (Ctrl-C) or respond to prompts.
   582→Warnings display as yellow text; fatals exit with non-zero status.
   583→
   584→[[axe_bash_unattended]]
   585→{axe_bash_unattended}::
   586→A
   587→{axl_motif}
   588→representing a bash script executing without human presence.
   589→Output captured to logs.
   590→No interactive prompts possible.
   591→Failures must be self-documenting for later analysis.
   592→
   593→[[axe_bash_scripted]]
   594→{axe_bash_scripted}::
   595→A
   596→{axl_motif}
   597→representing a bash script deliberately invoked to run to completion.
   598→No human interaction during execution.
   599→Succeeds atomically or fails with error status.
   600→Output may go to stdout/stderr for capture or display.
   601→Distinct from
   602→{axe_bash_unattended}
   603→which implies background/scheduled execution with log capture.
   604→
   605→[[axe_human_guide]]
   606→{axe_human_guide}::
   607→A
   608→{axl_motif}
   609→representing a human executing a specific rendered procedure document while interacting with an external system (web console, GUI application).
   610→The procedure document presents numbered steps; the human performs each in the external system.
   611→Feedback is visual confirmation in the external UI.
   612→Human judges success or failure of each step.
   613→Warnings are cautionary notes; fatals mean abort procedure.
   614→
   615→[[axe_rest_api]]
   616→{axe_rest_api}::
   617→A
   618→{axl_motif}
   619→representing invocation of an HTTP REST API endpoint.
   620→Request/response via JSON or other structured format.
   621→Authentication via bearer tokens, API keys, or similar.
   622→Calls return HTTP status codes and response bodies.
   623→
   624→[[axe_daemon_runtime]]
   625→{axe_daemon_runtime}::
   626→A
   627→{axl_motif}
   628→representing a long-running system process.
   629→Errors are logged, not displayed.
   630→Fatal means graceful shutdown with cleanup.
   631→No human in immediate feedback loop.
   632→
   633→== Axial Section Terms
   634→
   635→{axs_*} terms represent documentation sections for
   636→{axo_procedure}
   637→specifications.
   638→Each section motif captures a standard aspect of procedure documentation.
   639→{axl_voicing_s}
   640→adapt these abstract sections to interface-specific forms.
   641→
   642→[[axs_inputs]]
   643→{axs_inputs}::
   644→A
   645→{axl_motif}
   646→representing a section documenting what an
   647→{axo_procedure}
   648→requires to execute.
   649→Covers arguments, parameters, configuration, and input data.
   650→{axl_voicing_s}
   651→specify interface-appropriate input documentation:
   652→{axa_argument_list}
   653→for CLI,
   654→{axi_request_body}
   655→for REST APIs.
   656→
   657→[[axs_preconditions]]
   658→{axs_preconditions}::
   659→A
   660→{axl_motif}
   661→representing a section documenting environmental state requirements before
   662→{axo_procedure}
   663→execution.
   664→Optional section for procedures with significant prerequisites.
   665→Covers required services, authentication state, file system conditions, or resource availability.
   666→Distinct from
   667→{axs_inputs}
   668→which documents explicit parameters.
   669→
   670→[[axs_behavior]]
   671→{axs_behavior}::
   672→A
   673→{axl_motif}
   674→representing a section documenting what an
   675→{axo_procedure}
   676→does during execution.
   677→Describes the steps, actions, or transformations performed.
   678→{axl_voicing_s}
   679→may use
   680→{axo_sequence}
   681→patterns for ordered steps or prose for atomic operations.
   682→
   683→[[axs_outputs]]
   684→{axs_outputs}::
   685→A
   686→{axl_motif}
   687→representing a section documenting what an
   688→{axo_procedure}
   689→produces upon completion.
   690→Covers return values, created artifacts, modified state, or emitted data.
   691→{axl_voicing_s}
   692→specify interface-appropriate output documentation:
   693→{axi_response_body}
   694→for REST APIs, stdout/stderr patterns for CLI.
   695→
   696→[[axs_postconditions]]
   697→{axs_postconditions}::
   698→A
   699→{axl_motif}
   700→representing a section documenting environmental state guarantees after successful
   701→{axo_procedure}
   702→execution.
   703→Optional section for procedures with significant side effects.
   704→Covers created resources, modified permissions, or system state changes.
   705→Distinct from
   706→{axs_outputs}
   707→which documents explicit return values.
   708→
   709→[[axs_completion]]
   710→{axs_completion}::
   711→A
   712→{axl_motif}
   713→representing a section documenting how an
   714→{axo_procedure}
   715→signals success or failure.
   716→Covers exit codes, return status, completion indicators, or success criteria.
   717→{axl_voicing_s}
   718→specify interface-appropriate completion documentation:
   719→{axa_exit_uniform}
   720→or
   721→{axa_exit_enumerated}
   722→for CLI,
   723→HTTP status codes for REST APIs.
   724→
   725→[[axs_errors]]
   726→{axs_errors}::
   727→A
   728→{axl_motif}
   729→representing a section documenting failure modes of an
   730→{axo_procedure}.
   731→Optional section for procedures with non-obvious failure conditions.
   732→Covers error categories, recovery actions, or diagnostic guidance.
   733→Distinct from
   734→{axs_completion}
   735→which documents the signaling mechanism rather than failure semantics.
   736→
   737→=== Lifecycle Section Motifs
   738→
   739→These
   740→{axs_*}
   741→sections apply to specific lifecycle dimensions.
   742→
   743→[[axs_startup]]
   744→{axs_startup}::
   745→A
   746→{axl_motif}
   747→representing a section documenting initialization behavior for an
   748→{axd_longrunning}
   749→{axo_procedure}.
   750→Covers startup sequence, readiness criteria, and initialization dependencies.
   751→Required for
   752→{axd_longrunning}
   753→procedures.
   754→
   755→[[axs_shutdown]]
   756→{axs_shutdown}::
   757→A
   758→{axl_motif}
   759→representing a section documenting graceful termination for an
   760→{axd_longrunning}
   761→{axo_procedure}.
   762→Covers cleanup behavior, in-flight request handling, and resource release.
   763→Required for
   764→{axd_longrunning}
   765→procedures.
   766→
   767→[[axs_health]]
   768→{axs_health}::
   769→A
   770→{axl_motif}
   771→representing a section documenting health check semantics for an
   772→{axd_longrunning}
   773→{axo_procedure}.
   774→Covers liveness probes (is it running?), readiness probes (can it serve?), and health endpoints.
   775→Optional for
   776→{axd_longrunning}
   777→procedures.
   778→
   779→[[axs_signals]]
   780→{axs_signals}::
   781→A
   782→{axl_motif}
   783→representing a section documenting Unix signal handling for an
   784→{axo_procedure}.
   785→Covers SIGTERM (graceful stop), SIGHUP (reload config), SIGUSR1/2 (custom actions).
   786→Optional; most relevant for
   787→{axd_longrunning}
   788→but available to any
   789→{axo_command}.
   790→
   791→[[axs_recovery]]
   792→{axs_recovery}::
   793→A
   794→{axl_motif}
   795→representing a section documenting crash recovery behavior for an
   796→{axd_longrunning}
   797→{axo_procedure}.
   798→Covers restart behavior, state recovery, and failure mode handling.
   799→Optional for
   800→{axd_longrunning}
   801→procedures.
   802→
   803→[[axs_schedule]]
   804→{axs_schedule}::
   805→A
   806→{axl_motif}
   807→representing a section documenting timing specification for an
   808→{axd_periodic}
   809→{axo_procedure}.
   810→Covers cron expressions, intervals, or calendar-based triggers.
   811→Required for
   812→{axd_periodic}
   813→procedures.
   814→
   815→[[axs_idempotency]]
   816→{axs_idempotency}::
   817→A
   818→{axl_motif}
   819→representing a section documenting re-run safety for an
   820→{axd_periodic}
   821→{axo_procedure}.
   822→Covers duplicate handling, safe re-execution, and state management across runs.
   823→Optional for
   824→{axd_periodic}
   825→procedures.
   826→
   827→[[axs_overlap]]
   828→{axs_overlap}::
   829→A
   830→{axl_motif}
   831→representing a section documenting concurrent execution behavior for an
   832→{axd_periodic}
   833→{axo_procedure}.
   834→Covers what happens when a new run triggers while previous run is still executing.
   835→Options include: skip, queue, terminate previous, or allow parallel.
   836→Optional for
   837→{axd_periodic}
   838→procedures.
   839→
   840→== Axial Operation Terms
   841→
   842→=== Procedure Hierarchy
   843→
   844→{axo_procedure}
   845→is the superset
   846→{axl_motif}
   847→for procedural articulations.
   848→{axo_command},
   849→{axo_guide},
   850→and
   851→{axo_routine}
   852→are specialized forms that voice
   853→{axo_procedure}
   854→with additional constraints.
   855→
   856→{axo_sequence}
   857→is a structural characteristic describing HOW behavior is organized, not a procedure type.
   858→Most procedures ARE sequential.
   859→
   860→The following
   861→{axo_*}
   862→terms are NOT procedures and remain outside the hierarchy:
   863→{axo_entity},
   864→{axo_role},
   865→{axo_identity},
   866→{axo_actor},
   867→{axo_dependency}.
   868→
   869→[[axo_procedure]]
   870→{axo_procedure}::
   871→A
   872→{axl_motif}
   873→representing an executable operation with defined inputs, behavior, outputs, and completion semantics.
   874→{axo_procedure}
   875→is the superset for all procedural articulations.
   876→{axl_voicing_s}
   877→specialize by executor (machine vs human), reusability (terminal vs composite), and interface (CLI, REST, console).
   878→Documentation follows
   879→{axs_*}
   880→section patterns.
   881→
   882→[[axo_command]]
   883→{axo_command}::
   884→A
   885→{axl_motif}
   886→representing an
   887→{axo_procedure}
   888→executed by machine without human intervention.
   889→{axl_voices}
   890→{axo_procedure}
   891→with machine executor constraint.
   892→Requires
   893→{axe_environment}
   894→dimension specifying execution context.
   895→
   896→[[axo_guide]]
   897→{axo_guide}::
   898→A
   899→{axl_motif}
   900→representing an
   901→{axo_procedure}
   902→executed by a human operator following system-presented steps.
   903→{axl_voices}
   904→{axo_procedure}
   905→with human executor constraint.
   906→The system displays steps; the human performs them in external systems or consoles.
   907→Behavior steps use
   908→{axe_human_guide}
   909→control voicings.
   910→
   911→[[axo_routine]]
   912→{axo_routine}::
   913→A
   914→{axl_motif}
   915→representing a reusable
   916→{axo_procedure}
   917→invoked as a subsequence within other procedures.
   918→{axl_voices}
   919→{axo_procedure}
   920→with composite reusability constraint.
   921→Encapsulates common logic for consistent reuse across specifications.
   922→
   923→[[axo_sequence]]
   924→{axo_sequence}::
   925→A
   926→{axl_motif}
   927→representing a structural characteristic: ordered processing steps executed in defined progression.
   928→NOT a procedure type—describes HOW behavior is organized, not WHAT kind of procedure something is.
   929→Most
   930→{axo_procedure}
   931→voicings ARE sequential.
   932→Use within
   933→{axs_behavior}
   934→sections to document step ordering.
   935→
   936→[[axo_role]]
   937→{axo_role}::
   938→A
   939→{axl_motif}
   940→representing an operational identity pattern that defines capabilities and permissions.
   941→Determines available operations and access boundaries.
   942→
   943→[[axo_identity]]
   944→{axo_identity}::
   945→A
   946→{axl_motif}
   947→representing the abstract concept of an operational identity.
   948→An entity capable of authentication and holding authorities within a system.
   949→{axo_identity}
   950→is the pattern;
   951→{axo_actor_s}
   952→are named instantiations with specific bindings.
   953→
   954→[[axo_actor]]
   955→{axo_actor}::
   956→A
   957→{axl_motif}
   958→representing a specific named identity with defined responsibilities and exclusive authority over an operational domain.
   959→{axo_actor_s}
   960→instantiate
   961→{axo_identity}
   962→with concrete bindings (human via OAuth, service via service account) and permissions.
   963→
   964→[[axo_entity]]
   965→{axo_entity}::
   966→A
   967→{axl_motif}
   968→representing an object with both structure and behavior.
   969→Unlike
   970→{axr_record}
   971→which captures pure data,
   972→{axo_entity}
   973→includes methods, builder patterns, or lifecycle operations.
   974→{axl_voicing_s}
   975→describe the entity's data members and its behavioral interface (construction, mutation, registration).
   976→Typical manifestations include Rust structs with `impl` blocks, builder-pattern APIs, or objects that participate in registration protocols.
   977→
   978→== Axial Type Terms
   979→
   980→[[axt_type]]
   981→{axt_type}::
   982→A
   983→{axl_motif}
   984→representing a data format constraint that defines valid values and structures.
   985→Enforces data validation and enables type-safe operations.
   986→
   987→[[axt_string]]
   988→{axt_string}::
   989→A
   990→{axl_motif}
   991→representing text data type with optional pattern validation.
   992→{axl_voicing_s}
   993→may address context-specific concerns including:
   994→+
   995→* Encoding (ASCII, UTF-8, locale-dependent character sets)
   996→* Termination semantics (null-terminated, length-prefixed, delimiter-bounded)
   997→* Mutability (immutable values, mutable buffers)
   998→* Memory management (owned, borrowed, reference-counted)
   999→* Safety constraints (shell-safe, URL-safe, JSON-safe, SQL-safe)
  1000→* Format patterns (timestamps, identifiers, hashes, paths)
  1001→* Size and bounds (fixed-length, maximum length, unbounded)
  1002→
  1003→[[axt_number]]
  1004→{axt_number}::
  1005→A
  1006→{axl_motif}
  1007→representing numeric value type supporting integers and floating point.
  1008→May specify range constraints or precision requirements.
  1009→
  1010→[[axt_boolean]]
  1011→{axt_boolean}::
  1012→A
  1013→{axl_motif}
  1014→representing binary true/false value type.
  1015→Maps to language-specific boolean implementations.
  1016→
  1017→[[axt_array]]
  1018→{axt_array}::
  1019→A
  1020→{axl_motif}
  1021→representing ordered collection of typed elements.
  1022→Specifies element type and optional size constraints.
  1023→
  1024→[[axt_object]]
  1025→{axt_object}::
  1026→A
  1027→{axl_motif}
  1028→representing named field collection forming a composite type.
  1029→Contains typed fields with defined cardinality.
  1030→
  1031→[[axt_enumeration]]
  1032→{axt_enumeration}::
  1033→A
  1034→{axl_motif}
  1035→representing constrained set of valid string values.
  1036→Restricts values to predefined options.
  1037→
  1038→[[axt_enum_value]]
  1039→{axt_enum_value}::
  1040→A
  1041→{axl_motif}
  1042→representing single allowed option within an
  1043→{axt_enumeration}.
  1044→Must be explicitly listed in enumeration definition.
  1045→
  1046→[[axt_variant]]
  1047→{axt_variant}::
  1048→A
  1049→{axl_motif}
  1050→representing a value that can take one of several distinct forms, where each form may have different structure.
  1051→Unlike
  1052→{axt_enumeration}
  1053→which constrains to predefined strings,
  1054→{axt_variant}
  1055→allows structurally different alternatives.
  1056→{axl_voicing_s}
  1057→define the specific forms and discrimination logic for their context.
  1058→
  1059→== Axial Record Terms
  1060→
  1061→{axr_record_s}
  1062→and
  1063→{axr_member_s}
  1064→provide format-agnostic patterns for structured data.
  1065→Format-specific variants (JSON, protobuf, XML, etc.) capture serialization-specific semantics.
  1066→{axr_member_s}
  1067→inherit format context from their parent
  1068→{axr_record}.
  1069→
  1070→[[axr_record]]
  1071→{axr_record}::
  1072→A
  1073→{axl_motif}
  1074→representing a named collection of typed
  1075→{axr_member_s}.
  1076→Abstract pattern that format-specific variants voice with serialization semantics.
  1077→Use format-specific variants (
  1078→{axr_record_json},
  1079→{axr_record_protobuf},
  1080→etc.) when format is known.
  1081→
  1082→[[axr_record_json]]
  1083→{axr_record_json}::
  1084→A
  1085→{axl_motif}
  1086→representing a JSON object structure.
  1087→{axr_member_s}
  1088→are unordered, string-keyed properties.
  1089→Requires
  1090→{axd_nullable}
  1091→specification since JSON has explicit null.
  1092→
  1093→[[axr_record_protobuf]]
  1094→{axr_record_protobuf}::
  1095→A
  1096→{axl_motif}
  1097→representing a protobuf message.
  1098→{axr_member_s}
  1099→require
  1100→{axd_field_number}
  1101→and
  1102→{axd_wire_type}
  1103→dimensions.
  1104→Supports schema evolution through field numbering.
  1105→
  1106→[[axr_record_xml]]
  1107→{axr_record_xml}::
  1108→A
  1109→{axl_motif}
  1110→representing an XML element structure.
  1111→{axr_member_s}
  1112→require
  1113→{axd_xml_form}
  1114→to distinguish attributes from child elements.
  1115→Order may be significant depending on schema.
  1116→
  1117→[[axr_record_yaml]]
  1118→{axr_record_yaml}::
  1119→A
  1120→{axl_motif}
  1121→representing a YAML mapping.
  1122→Superset of JSON with anchors, aliases, and multi-document support.
  1123→{axr_member_s}
  1124→follow JSON-like semantics with extended syntax.
  1125→
  1126→[[axr_record_binary]]
  1127→{axr_record_binary}::
  1128→A
  1129→{axl_motif}
  1130→representing a binary/memory structure.
  1131→{axr_member_s}
  1132→require
  1133→{axd_endianness}
  1134→for multi-byte fields.
  1135→Fixed layout with explicit size and alignment.
  1136→
  1137→[[axr_member]]
  1138→{axr_member}::
  1139→A
  1140→{axl_motif}
  1141→representing a named, typed element within an
  1142→{axr_record}.
  1143→Inherits serialization format from parent
  1144→{axr_record}.
  1145→Qualified by
  1146→{axd_dimension_s}
  1147→in
  1148→{axl_annotation}:
  1149→`// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_nullable⟧`
  1150→
  1151→== Axial JSON Terms
  1152→
  1153→[[axj_structure]]
  1154→{axj_structure}::
  1155→A
  1156→{axl_motif}
  1157→representing named schema definition for JSON objects.
  1158→Defines complete object shape with field specifications.
  1159→
  1160→[[axj_field]]
  1161→{axj_field}::
  1162→A
  1163→{axl_motif}
  1164→representing named, typed element within a
  1165→{axj_structure}.
  1166→Combines tag identifier with type constraint.
  1167→
  1168→[[axj_field_tag]]
  1169→{axj_field_tag}::
  1170→A
  1171→{axl_motif}
  1172→representing the key identifier for a
  1173→{axj_field}.
  1174→Follows naming conventions of target specification.
  1175→
  1176→[[axj_field_type]]
  1177→{axj_field_type}::
  1178→A
  1179→{axl_motif}
  1180→representing the value constraint for a
  1181→{axj_field}.
  1182→Determines parsing and validation behavior.
  1183→
  1184→[[axj_array_element]]
  1185→{axj_array_element}::
  1186→A
  1187→{axl_motif}
  1188→representing type constraint for members of an
  1189→{axt_array}
  1190→field.
  1191→Applies uniformly to all array elements.
  1192→
  1193→[[axj_nested_field]]
  1194→{axj_nested_field}::
  1195→A
  1196→{axl_motif}
  1197→representing fields within object-typed fields enabling hierarchy.
  1198→Maintains type safety through nested validation.
  1199→
  1200→[[axj_root_structure]]
  1201→{axj_root_structure}::
  1202→A
  1203→{axl_motif}
  1204→representing top-level JSON object schema.
  1205→Entry point for JSON document validation.
  1206→
  1207→[[axj_field_cardinality]]
  1208→{axj_field_cardinality}::
  1209→A
  1210→{axl_motif}
  1211→representing required, optional, or repeated nature of a field.
  1212→Maps to validation rules in implementation.
  1213→
  1214→[[axj_type_reference]]
  1215→{axj_type_reference}::
  1216→A
  1217→{axl_motif}
  1218→representing pointer to another
  1219→{axj_structure}
  1220→for composition.
  1221→Maintains referential integrity across definitions.
  1222→
  1223→== Axial Argument Terms
  1224→
  1225→[[axa_argument]]
  1226→{axa_argument}::
  1227→A
  1228→{axl_motif}
  1229→representing command parameter accepting a value.
  1230→Foundation for CLI and function interfaces.
  1231→
  1232→[[axa_positional]]
  1233→{axa_positional}::
  1234→A
  1235→{axl_motif}
  1236→representing order-determines-meaning parameter in command invocation.
  1237→Position in argument list defines semantic role.
  1238→
  1239→[[axa_keyword]]
  1240→{axa_keyword}::
  1241→A
  1242→{axl_motif}
  1243→representing name-determines-meaning parameter with explicit identifier.
  1244→Order-independent with explicit naming.
  1245→
  1246→[[axa_long_option]]
  1247→{axa_long_option}::
  1248→A
  1249→{axl_motif}
  1250→representing keyword argument with `--` prefix following GNU conventions.
  1251→Uses hyphenated multi-word names.
  1252→
  1253→[[axa_short_option]]
  1254→{axa_short_option}::
  1255→A
  1256→{axl_motif}
  1257→representing keyword argument with single `-` prefix for brevity.
  1258→Often provides shorthand for
  1259→{axa_long_option}.
  1260→
  1261→[[axa_option_name]]
  1262→{axa_option_name}::
  1263→A
  1264→{axl_motif}
  1265→representing the identifier portion of a keyword argument.
  1266→Defines the semantic meaning of the option.
  1267→
  1268→[[axa_option_value]]
  1269→{axa_option_value}::
  1270→A
  1271→{axl_motif}
  1272→representing the data portion following an option identifier.
  1273→Subject to
  1274→{axa_argument_type}
  1275→constraints.
  1276→
  1277→[[axa_argument_type]]
  1278→{axa_argument_type}::
  1279→A
  1280→{axl_motif}
  1281→representing expected data format for an
  1282→{axa_argument}.
  1283→Enforces type safety at interface boundary.
  1284→
  1285→[[axa_required]]
  1286→{axa_required}::
  1287→A
  1288→{axl_motif}
  1289→representing attribute indicating an
  1290→{axa_argument}
  1291→must be provided.
  1292→Absence triggers
  1293→{axc_fatal}
  1294→or error response.
  1295→
  1296→[[axa_optional]]
  1297→{axa_optional}::
  1298→A
  1299→{axl_motif}
  1300→representing attribute indicating an
  1301→{axa_argument}
  1302→may be omitted.
  1303→Provides flexibility in interface usage.
  1304→
  1305→[[axa_defaulted]]
  1306→{axa_defaulted}::
  1307→A
  1308→{axl_motif}
  1309→representing attribute indicating an
  1310→{axa_argument}
  1311→has a fallback value.
  1312→Specifies behavior when
  1313→{axa_optional}
  1314→argument omitted.
  1315→
  1316→[[axa_argument_list]]
  1317→{axa_argument_list}::
  1318→A
  1319→{axl_motif}
  1320→representing a structured section documenting an operation's arguments.
  1321→{axl_voices}
  1322→{axs_inputs}
  1323→in CLI context.
  1324→Within an
  1325→{axa_argument_list},
  1326→{axd_dimension}
  1327→annotations may precede bullet items to qualify argument usage without requiring anchors.
  1328→Each item references a globally-defined argument term.
  1329→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1330→+
  1331→Example section header definition:
  1332→+
  1333→[source,asciidoc]
  1334→----
  1335→[[jjds_arguments]]
  1336→// ⟦axl_voices axa_argument_list⟧
  1337→{jjds_arguments}::
  1338→Section header introducing operation arguments.
  1339→----
  1340→+
  1341→Example usage in an operation:
  1342→+
  1343→[source,asciidoc]
  1344→----
  1345→{jjds_arguments}
  1346→
  1347→// ⟦axd_required⟧
  1348→* {jjda_silks}
  1349→
  1350→// ⟦axd_optional axd_defaulted⟧
  1351→* {jjda_file}
  1352→----
  1353→
  1354→[[axa_cli_option]]
  1355→{axa_cli_option}::
  1356→A
  1357→{axl_motif}
  1358→representing a command-line option definition.
  1359→{axl_voicing_s}
  1360→require the definition text to specify:
  1361→+
  1362→* The long form (e.g., `--file`)
  1363→* The short form, or its absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
  1364→+
  1365→Example:
  1366→+
  1367→[source,asciidoc]
  1368→----
  1369→[[jjda_file]]
  1370→// ⟦axl_voices axa_cli_option⟧
  1371→{jjda_file}::
  1372→Path to the Gallops JSON file.
  1373→Long: `--file`.
  1374→Short: none.
  1375→Default: `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
  1376→----
  1377→
  1378→[[axa_exit_uniform]]
  1379→{axa_exit_uniform}::
  1380→A
  1381→{axl_motif}
  1382→representing an Exit Status section that follows standard Unix conventions.
  1383→{axl_voices}
  1384→{axs_completion}
  1385→in CLI context.
  1386→Exit 0 indicates success; any non-zero exit indicates error.
  1387→{axl_voicing_s}
  1388→may use terse notation since the pattern is conventional.
  1389→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1390→Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
  1391→+
  1392→Example section header definition:
  1393→+
  1394→[source,asciidoc]
  1395→----
  1396→[[jjds_exit_uniform]]
  1397→// ⟦axl_voices axa_exit_uniform⟧
  1398→{jjds_exit_uniform}::
  1399→Section header for operations with standard exit semantics.
  1400→----
  1401→+
  1402→Example usage:
  1403→+
  1404→[source,asciidoc]
  1405→----
  1406→{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  1407→----
  1408→
  1409→[[axa_exit_enumerated]]
  1410→{axa_exit_enumerated}::
  1411→A
  1412→{axl_motif}
  1413→representing an Exit Status section with distinct exit codes carrying specific meanings.
  1414→{axl_voices}
  1415→{axs_completion}
  1416→in CLI context.
  1417→{axl_voicing_s}
  1418→require the definition text to list each exit code and its meaning.
  1419→The voicing is placed on the section header term definition, not repeated at each usage.
  1420→Specifications define separate section header terms for uniform vs enumerated exit patterns.
  1421→+
  1422→Example section header definition:
  1423→+
  1424→[source,asciidoc]
  1425→----
  1426→[[jjds_exit_enumerated]]
  1427→// ⟦axl_voices axa_exit_enumerated⟧
  1428→{jjds_exit_enumerated}::
  1429→Section header for operations with distinct exit codes.
  1430→----
  1431→+
  1432→Example usage:
  1433→+
  1434→[source,asciidoc]
  1435→----
  1436→{jjds_exit_enumerated}
  1437→
  1438→* 0 — found and valid
  1439→* 1 — not found
  1440→* 2 — found but malformed
  1441→----
  1442→
  1443→== Axial Interface Terms
  1444→
  1445→[[axi_command_interface]]
  1446→{axi_command_interface}::
  1447→A
  1448→{axl_motif}
  1449→representing complete specification of a command's invocation pattern.
  1450→Defines syntax and semantics for command usage.
  1451→
  1452→[[axi_subcommand]]
  1453→{axi_subcommand}::
  1454→A
  1455→{axl_motif}
  1456→representing nested command within parent command structure.
  1457→Inherits context from parent command.
  1458→
  1459→[[axi_command_signature]]
  1460→{axi_command_signature}::
  1461→A
  1462→{axl_motif}
  1463→representing complete parameter specification for a command.
  1464→Serves as contract for command invocation.
  1465→
  1466→[[axi_endpoint]]
  1467→{axi_endpoint}::
  1468→A
  1469→{axl_motif}
  1470→representing URL path accepting HTTP requests.
  1471→Defines resource location and accepted operations.
  1472→
  1473→[[axi_api_method]]
  1474→{axi_api_method}::
  1475→A
  1476→{axl_motif}
  1477→representing HTTP verb combined with
  1478→{axi_endpoint}
  1479→specification.
  1480→Includes request and response specifications.
  1481→
  1482→[[axi_url_parameter]]
  1483→{axi_url_parameter}::
  1484→A
  1485→{axl_motif}
  1486→representing query string key-value pair in URL.
  1487→Appears after `?` in URL structure.
  1488→
  1489→[[axi_hash_parameter]]
  1490→{axi_hash_parameter}::
  1491→A
  1492→{axl_motif}
  1493→representing fragment identifier for client-side state.
  1494→Persists state without server round-trips.
  1495→
  1496→[[axi_request_body]]
  1497→{axi_request_body}::
  1498→A
  1499→{axl_motif}
  1500→representing structured input payload for HTTP requests.
  1501→Validated against
  1502→{axj_structure}
  1503→definitions.
  1504→
  1505→[[axi_response_body]]
  1506→{axi_response_body}::
  1507→A
  1508→{axl_motif}
  1509→representing structured output payload from HTTP responses.
  1510→Conforms to documented
  1511→{axj_structure}.
  1512→
  1513→[[axi_cli_interface]]
  1514→{axi_cli_interface}::
  1515→A
  1516→{axl_motif}
  1517→representing command-line invocation pattern specification.
  1518→Documents
  1519→{axa_argument_s}
  1520→and behavior.
  1521→
  1522→[[axi_cli_program]]
  1523→{axi_cli_program}::
  1524→A
  1525→{axl_motif}
  1526→representing a Unix-style command-line program.
  1527→Invoked from a shell with arguments.
  1528→Writes results to stdout, errors to stderr.
  1529→Returns exit status: 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
  1530→Suitable for scripted, non-interactive use.
  1531→
  1532→[[axi_cli_command_group]]
  1533→{axi_cli_command_group}::
  1534→A
  1535→{axl_motif}
  1536→representing a namespace within a CLI that contains subcommands.
  1537→Does not perform operations itself; serves as organizational container.
  1538→May be nested within an
  1539→{axi_cli_program}
  1540→or another
  1541→{axi_cli_command_group}.
  1542→Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent.
  1543→
  1544→[[axi_cli_subcommand]]
  1545→{axi_cli_subcommand}::
  1546→A
  1547→{axl_motif}
  1548→representing a leaf operation within an
  1549→{axi_cli_program}
  1550→or
  1551→{axi_cli_command_group}.
  1552→Performs an actual operation; not a container for other commands.
  1553→Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent: arguments, stdout, stderr, exit status.
  1554→
  1555→[[axi_rest_api]]
  1556→{axi_rest_api}::
  1557→A
  1558→{axl_motif}
  1559→representing resource-based web service interface.
  1560→Maps operations to HTTP methods and resources.
  1561→
  1562→[[axi_rpc_interface]]
  1563→{axi_rpc_interface}::
  1564→A
  1565→{axl_motif}
  1566→representing remote procedure call pattern for service invocation.
  1567→Emphasizes operation semantics over HTTP methods.
  1568→
  1569→[[axi_sse_endpoint]]
  1570→{axi_sse_endpoint}::
  1571→A
  1572→{axl_motif}
  1573→representing server-sent event stream for push notifications.
  1574→Enables real-time updates to connected clients.
  1575→
  1576→[[axi_cc_slash_command]]
  1577→{axi_cc_slash_command}::
  1578→A
  1579→{axl_motif}
  1580→representing a Claude Code slash command skill artifact.
  1581→Implemented as a `.claude/commands/*.md` file containing orchestration logic that the LLM executes as a skill.
  1582→Slash commands coordinate multiple
  1583→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  1584→invocations, gather context, and apply workflow policies.
  1585→
  1586→[[axi_cc_claudemd_verb]]
  1587→{axi_cc_claudemd_verb}::
  1588→A
  1589→{axl_motif}
  1590→representing an action verb defined in a
  1591→{axi_cc_claudemd_section}
  1592→that maps user intent to tool-layer operations.
  1593→The verb exists as a concept in the LLM context — either mapping directly to a single
  1594→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  1595→(direct verb) or orchestrating multiple operations (multistep verb implemented by a
  1596→{axi_cc_slash_command}).
  1597→
  1598→[[axi_cc_claudemd_section]]
  1599→{axi_cc_claudemd_section}::
  1600→A
  1601→{axl_motif}
  1602→representing a managed section within a project CLAUDE.md file.
  1603→Provides persistent LLM context that bridges specification-layer concepts to runtime execution.
  1604→Typically contains command references, vocabulary mappings, and operational guidance.
  1605→
  1606→== Axial Dimension Terms
  1607→
  1608→{axd_dimension_s}
  1609→are modifiers that appear in
  1610→{axl_annotation_s}
  1611→to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
  1612→Unlike primary
  1613→{axl_motif_s},
  1614→{axd_dimension_s}
  1615→do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
  1616→Applicability of
  1617→{axd_dimension_s}
  1618→depends on serialization format context.
  1619→
  1620→[[axd_dimension]]
  1621→{axd_dimension}::
  1622→A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
  1623→{axl_annotation}.
  1624→{axd_dimension_s}
  1625→appear after the primary
  1626→{axl_motif}
  1627→in annotation format: `// ⟦axl_voices primary dimension ...⟧`
  1628→
  1629→=== Value Dimensions
  1630→
  1631→These
  1632→{axd_dimension_s}
  1633→describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
  1634→They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).
  1635→
  1636→[[axd_required]]
  1637→{axd_required}::
  1638→Member or argument must be present.
  1639→Absence triggers validation error or
  1640→{axc_fatal}.
  1641→
  1642→[[axd_optional]]
  1643→{axd_optional}::
  1644→Member or argument may be omitted.
  1645→Absence is valid and handled gracefully.
  1646→
  1647→[[axd_nullable]]
  1648→{axd_nullable}::
  1649→Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
  1650→Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
  1651→Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.
  1652→
  1653→[[axd_none]]
  1654→{axd_none}::
  1655→Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
  1656→Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
  1657→Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.
  1658→
  1659→[[axd_defaulted]]
  1660→{axd_defaulted}::
  1661→Has fallback value when absent.
  1662→Voicing should specify the default value in prose.
  1663→
  1664→[[axd_repeated]]
  1665→{axd_repeated}::
  1666→May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
  1667→Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
  1668→Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.
  1669→
  1670→[[axd_list]]
  1671→{axd_list}::
  1672→Single value containing delimited multiple values.
  1673→Distinct from
  1674→{axd_repeated}
  1675→which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
  1676→Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
  1677→Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).
  1678→
  1679→=== Format-Specific Dimensions
  1680→
  1681→These
  1682→{axd_dimension_s}
  1683→apply only to specific serialization formats.
  1684→Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.
  1685→
  1686→[[axd_field_number]]
  1687→{axd_field_number}::
  1688→Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
  1689→Required for protobuf format.
  1690→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
  1691→
  1692→[[axd_wire_type]]
  1693→{axd_wire_type}::
  1694→Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
  1695→Required for protobuf format.
  1696→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
  1697→
  1698→[[axd_endianness]]
  1699→{axd_endianness}::
  1700→Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
  1701→Required for binary format.
  1702→Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.
  1703→
  1704→[[axd_xml_form]]
  1705→{axd_xml_form}::
  1706→Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
  1707→Required for XML format.
  1708→Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.
  1709→
  1710→=== Lifecycle Dimensions
  1711→
  1712→These
  1713→{axd_dimension_s}
  1714→qualify
  1715→{axo_procedure}
  1716→voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
  1717→Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
  1718→{axo_procedure}
  1719→voicing.
  1720→Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
  1721→
  1722→[[axd_transient]]
  1723→{axd_transient}::
  1724→{axo_procedure}
  1725→executes and completes.
  1726→The default lifecycle for most procedures.
  1727→{axs_completion}
  1728→documents exit status or return value.
  1729→
  1730→[[axd_longrunning]]
  1731→{axd_longrunning}::
  1732→{axo_procedure}
  1733→runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
  1734→{axs_completion}
  1735→documents startup success criteria, not termination.
  1736→Requires
  1737→{axs_startup}
  1738→and
  1739→{axs_shutdown}
  1740→sections.
  1741→May include
  1742→{axs_health},
  1743→{axs_signals},
  1744→{axs_recovery}
  1745→sections.
  1746→
  1747→[[axd_periodic]]
  1748→{axd_periodic}::
  1749→{axo_procedure}
  1750→executes on a schedule.
  1751→{axs_completion}
  1752→applies per-run.
  1753→Requires
  1754→{axs_schedule}
  1755→section.
  1756→May include
  1757→{axs_idempotency},
  1758→{axs_overlap}
  1759→sections.
  1760→
  1761→=== Regime Dimensions
  1762→
  1763→These
  1764→{axd_dimension_s}
  1765→qualify regime structure annotations.
  1766→
  1767→[[axd_conditional]]
  1768→{axd_conditional}::
  1769→Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
  1770→A group annotated with
  1771→{axd_conditional}
  1772→requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
  1773→{axhrgc_gate}
  1774→specifying the activation criteria.
  1775→
  1776→=== Dimension Applicability
  1777→
  1778→Per-format guidance for
  1779→{axd_dimension}
  1780→usage:
  1781→
  1782→[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
  1783→|===
  1784→|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML
  1785→
  1786→|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1787→|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1788→|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
  1789→|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
  1790→|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
  1791→|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
  1792→|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
  1793→|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
  1794→|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
  1795→|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
  1796→|===
  1797→
  1798→Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.
  1799→
  1800→== Axial Format Terms
  1801→
  1802→{axf_format_s}
  1803→specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
  1804→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1805→use a particular
  1806→{axf_format}
  1807→to encode
  1808→{axrg_variable}
  1809→values in files that can be sourced, included, or parsed.
  1810→
  1811→[[axf_format]]
  1812→{axf_format}::
  1813→A
  1814→{axl_motif}
  1815→representing a serialization encoding for configuration data.
  1816→{axf_format_s}
  1817→determine how
  1818→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1819→are written to and read from files.
  1820→
  1821→[[axf_bash]]
  1822→{axf_bash}::
  1823→A
  1824→{axl_motif}
  1825→representing bash script format for configuration.
  1826→Files are source-able via `. file` or `source file`.
  1827→Variables declared as `VAR=value` without export, or `export VAR=value` for environment propagation.
  1828→
  1829→[[axf_makefile]]
  1830→{axf_makefile}::
  1831→A
  1832→{axl_motif}
  1833→representing makefile format for configuration.
  1834→Files are includable via `include file`.
  1835→Variables declared as `VAR := value` for immediate expansion or `VAR = value` for deferred expansion.
  1836→
  1837→[[axf_json]]
  1838→{axf_json}::
  1839→A
  1840→{axl_motif}
  1841→representing JSON format for configuration.
  1842→Standard JSON object structure with string keys mapping to typed values.
  1843→Parsed by standard JSON libraries.
  1844→
  1845→[[axf_yaml]]
  1846→{axf_yaml}::
  1847→A
  1848→{axl_motif}
  1849→representing YAML format for configuration.
  1850→Superset of JSON with additional syntax for readability.
  1851→Parsed by YAML libraries with attention to version-specific semantics.
  1852→
  1853→== Axial Regime Terms
  1854→
  1855→{axrg_regime_s}
  1856→are structured configuration systems that coordinate specification, validation, and rendering of typed configuration data.
  1857→
  1858→[[axrg_regime]]
  1859→{axrg_regime}::
  1860→A
  1861→{axl_motif}
  1862→representing a structured configuration system.
  1863→A
  1864→{axrg_regime}
  1865→binds related
  1866→{axrg_variable_s}
  1867→under a namespace prefix, with coordinated specification, validation, and rendering components.
  1868→The specification defines what can be configured;
  1869→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1870→provide specific values.
  1871→
  1872→[[axrg_variable]]
  1873→{axrg_variable}::
  1874→A
  1875→{axl_motif}
  1876→representing a named configuration element at schema level.
  1877→Each
  1878→{axrg_variable}
  1879→belongs to an
  1880→{axrg_regime}
  1881→and defines a name, type constraints, and relationships with other variables.
  1882→{axrg_variable_s}
  1883→are the "what can be configured" definitions;
  1884→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1885→bind values to them.
  1886→
  1887→[[axrg_assignment]]
  1888→{axrg_assignment}::
  1889→A
  1890→{axl_motif}
  1891→representing a specific value bound to an
  1892→{axrg_variable}.
  1893→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1894→are carried in files of a particular
  1895→{axf_format}
  1896→(bash, makefile, JSON, etc.).
  1897→Multiple assignment files may exist for the same
  1898→{axrg_regime},
  1899→each providing different configuration instances.
  1900→
  1901→[[axrg_prefix]]
  1902→{axrg_prefix}::
  1903→A
  1904→{axl_motif}
  1905→representing the stable character sequence that namespaces all
  1906→{axrg_variable_s}
  1907→within an
  1908→{axrg_regime}.
  1909→Typically composed of a project marker and regime marker (e.g., `RBRR_` = `RB` + `RR` + `_`).
  1910→Once established, an
  1911→{axrg_prefix}
  1912→becomes deeply embedded across code, configuration files, and documentation.
  1913→Changing an
  1914→{axrg_prefix}
  1915→requires coordinated refactoring across all consuming artifacts.
  1916→
  1917→== Axial Regime Provenance Dimensions
  1918→
  1919→Provenance dimensions qualify
  1920→{axrg_assignment}
  1921→to declare how values arrive at
  1922→{axvr_broach}
  1923→time.
  1924→These are valid dimensions on
  1925→{axvr_regime}
  1926→declaring which provenance(s) a regime supports.
  1927→
  1928→[[axrd_file_sourced]]
  1929→{axrd_file_sourced}::
  1930→Assignments loaded from a persistent file via source or equivalent mechanism.
  1931→The file path is known at load time and the regime module is responsible for locating and sourcing it.
  1932→
  1933→[[axrd_env_inherited]]
  1934→{axrd_env_inherited}::
  1935→Assignments received as environment variables from a parent process.
  1936→The regime module finds variables already in scope; no file sourcing occurs.
  1937→
  1938→[[axrd_constructed]]
  1939→{axrd_constructed}::
  1940→Assignments computed or set programmatically before broach.
  1941→Values are assembled by application logic rather than loaded from a static source.
  1942→
  1943→== Axial Regime Annotation Terms
  1944→
  1945→Regime annotations use the prefix-discriminated form (`//ax...` with no space after `//`) to provide structured metadata about regime definitions in AsciiDoc specifications.
  1946→Two complementary annotation families enable regime specification across parent documents and their subdocuments.
  1947→
  1948→=== Definition-Site Voicing Annotations (axvr_*)
  1949→
  1950→{axvr_*} annotations appear in parent documents between anchor and definition, declaring the regime role of the term being defined.
  1951→The surface syntax follows the prefix-discriminated pattern:
  1952→
  1953→[source,asciidoc]
  1954→----
  1955→[[rbrv_sigil]]
  1956→//axvr_variable axd_required rbst_xname
  1957→{rbrv_sigil}::
  1958→Vessel identifier for build configurations.
  1959→----
  1960→
  1961→The annotation line starts with `//axvr_` (no space after `//`), followed by the specific voicing term, then space-separated dimension and type terms.
  1962→The anchor immediately preceding the annotation must match the first
  1963→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  1964→in the definition text that follows.
  1965→
  1966→[[axvr_regime]]
  1967→{axvr_regime}::
  1968→Voices a regime definition.
  1969→The annotated term defines a structured configuration system that binds related
  1970→{axrg_variable_s}
  1971→under a namespace prefix.
  1972→+
  1973→{axd_dimension_s}:
  1974→{axf_format}
  1975→(specifying how
  1976→{axrg_assignment_s}
  1977→are carried).
  1978→
  1979→[[axvr_variable]]
  1980→{axvr_variable}::
  1981→Voices a regime variable definition.
  1982→The annotated term defines a named configuration element within a regime.
  1983→The definition text must include, as its second
  1984→{mcm_attribute_reference},
  1985→the parent regime (an anchor that voices
  1986→{axrg_regime}).
  1987→This ties each variable unequivocally to its regime.
  1988→+
  1989→{axd_dimension_s}:
  1990→{axd_required}
  1991→or
  1992→{axd_optional},
  1993→type
  1994→{axl_motif}
  1995→(base type or type voicing).
  1996→
  1997→[[axvr_group]]
  1998→{axvr_group}::
  1999→Voices a regime group definition.
  2000→The annotated term defines a named grouping of related
  2001→{axrg_variable_s}
  2002→within a regime.
  2003→The definition text must include, as its second
  2004→{mcm_attribute_reference},
  2005→the parent regime (an anchor that voices
  2006→{axrg_regime}).
  2007→+
  2008→{axd_dimension_s}:
  2009→{axd_conditional}
  2010→(group activation depends on gate conditions in subdocument).
  2011→
  2012→[[axvr_broach]]
  2013→{axvr_broach}::
  2014→Voices a regime broach operation definition.
  2015→The annotated term defines the silent initialization phase for a
  2016→{axrg_regime}:
  2017→setting defaults, detecting unexpected variables, and building derived structures.
  2018→
  2019→[[axvr_validate]]
  2020→{axvr_validate}::
  2021→Voices a regime validate operation definition.
  2022→The annotated term defines the strict verification phase for a
  2023→{axrg_regime}:
  2024→checking all type and constraint requirements, failing on first violation.
  2025→
  2026→[[axvr_render]]
  2027→{axvr_render}::
  2028→Voices a regime render operation definition.
  2029→The annotated term defines the diagnostic display phase for a
  2030→{axrg_regime}:
  2031→producing human-readable presentation of current state.
  2032→Rendering occurs before validation so all fields are visible on failure.
  2033→
  2034→=== Standalone Hierarchy Markers (axhr*_)
  2035→
  2036→Hierarchy markers appear in regime subdocuments as standalone annotations, NOT between anchor and definition.
  2037→Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
  2038→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2039→from the following text to establish hierarchical context.
  2040→
  2041→The surface syntax:
  2042→
  2043→[source,asciidoc]
  2044→----
  2045→//axhrb_regime
  2046→{rbrv_regime}
  2047→
  2048→//axhrgv_variable
  2049→{rbrv_sigil}
  2050→----
  2051→
  2052→The hierarchy marker line starts with `//axhr` (no space after `//`), followed by the sub-prefix identifying the marker type.
  2053→Following text contains the
  2054→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2055→that the marker reads ahead.
  2056→
  2057→==== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
  2058→
  2059→[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
  2060→|===
  2061→|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
  2062→
  2063→|{axhrb_regime}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The regime this subdoc describes
  2064→|{axhrv_variable}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |An ungrouped regime variable
  2065→|{axhrgb_group}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A group (must match {axvr_group} in parent)
  2066→|{axhrgc_gate}      |2 {mcm_attribute_reference_s}|Enumerated variable + enum value
  2067→|{axhrgv_variable}  |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A variable within current group
  2068→|{axhro_broach}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level broach operation for this regime
  2069→|{axhro_validate}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level validate operation for this regime
  2070→|{axhro_render}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |Kit-level render operation for this regime
  2071→|===
  2072→
  2073→[[axhrb_regime]]
  2074→{axhrb_regime}::
  2075→Marks the regime that a subdocument describes.
  2076→Lookahead: 1
  2077→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2078→— the regime this subdoc describes.
  2079→The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
  2080→{axvr_regime}
  2081→in the parent document.
  2082→
  2083→[[axhrv_variable]]
  2084→{axhrv_variable}::
  2085→Marks an ungrouped regime variable in a subdocument.
  2086→Lookahead: 1
  2087→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2088→— the variable being detailed.
  2089→
  2090→[[axhrgb_group]]
  2091→{axhrgb_group}::
  2092→Marks a group boundary within a subdocument.
  2093→Lookahead: 1
  2094→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2095→— the group being detailed.
  2096→The referenced attribute must match an anchor annotated with
  2097→{axvr_group}
  2098→in the parent document.
  2099→
  2100→[[axhrgc_gate]]
  2101→{axhrgc_gate}::
  2102→Marks a conditional activation gate within a group.
  2103→Lookahead: 2
  2104→{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
  2105→— the enumerated variable (voicing
  2106→{axt_enumeration})
  2107→and the specific
  2108→{axt_enum_value}
  2109→that activates this gate.
  2110→The gate variable may belong to a different regime (cross-regime gating is legal).
  2111→
  2112→[[axhrgv_variable]]
  2113→{axhrgv_variable}::
  2114→Marks a variable within the current group in a subdocument.
  2115→Lookahead: 1
  2116→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2117→— the variable being detailed within the current group scope.
  2118→
  2119→[[axhro_broach]]
  2120→{axhro_broach}::
  2121→Marks a regime broach operation description in a subdocument.
  2122→Lookahead: 1
  2123→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2124→— the kit-level broach operation term (which must be voiced
  2125→{axvr_broach}
  2126→in the parent document).
  2127→
  2128→[[axhro_validate]]
  2129→{axhro_validate}::
  2130→Marks a regime validate operation description in a subdocument.
  2131→Lookahead: 1
  2132→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2133→— the kit-level validate operation term (which must be voiced
  2134→{axvr_validate}
  2135→in the parent document).
  2136→
  2137→[[axhro_render]]
  2138→{axhro_render}::
  2139→Marks a regime render operation description in a subdocument.
  2140→Lookahead: 1
  2141→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2142→— the kit-level render operation term (which must be voiced
  2143→{axvr_render}
  2144→in the parent document).
  2145→
  2146→==== Nesting Rules
  2147→
  2148→Hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
  2149→
  2150→* Higher-level markers implicitly close previous scope (no end markers needed)
  2151→* {axhrgb_group},
  2152→{axhrgc_gate},
  2153→and
  2154→{axhrgv_variable}
  2155→must appear within
  2156→{axhrb_regime}
  2157→scope
  2158→* {axhrgc_gate}
  2159→and
  2160→{axhrgv_variable}
  2161→must appear within
  2162→{axhrgb_group}
  2163→scope
  2164→* Multiple
  2165→{axhrgc_gate}
  2166→within one group have AND semantics
  2167→* {axhro_broach},
  2168→{axhro_validate},
  2169→and
  2170→{axhro_render}
  2171→must appear within
  2172→{axhrb_regime}
  2173→scope but outside any
  2174→{axhrgb_group}
  2175→scope
  2176→
  2177→==== Cross-Document Validation
  2178→
  2179→* {axhrb_regime}
  2180→attribute reference must match a regime defined with
  2181→{axvr_regime}
  2182→in parent
  2183→* {axhrgb_group}
  2184→attribute reference must match a group defined with
  2185→{axvr_group}
  2186→under that regime
  2187→* {axhrgc_gate}
  2188→first attribute reference must voice
  2189→{axt_enumeration};
  2190→second must be an
  2191→{axt_enum_value}
  2192→of that specific enumeration type
  2193→
  2194→== Axial Type Universal Terms
  2195→
  2196→{axtu_*} types represent generic infrastructure patterns that are not specific to any cloud provider or platform.
  2197→These types capture critical gestalts that appear across many systems.
  2198→Platform-specific nuances (e.g., path separators on Windows vs Unix) are handled through
  2199→{axl_voicing_s}.
  2200→
  2201→[[axtu_string]]
  2202→{axtu_string}::
  2203→A
  2204→{axl_motif}
  2205→representing a generic bounded string value.
  2206→{axl_voicing_s}
  2207→specify minimum and maximum length constraints appropriate to their context.
  2208→
  2209→[[axtu_path]]
  2210→{axtu_path}::
  2211→A
  2212→{axl_motif}
  2213→representing a file system path.
  2214→Platform differences (path separators, root conventions) are handled by
  2215→{axl_voicing_s}.
  2216→May be absolute or relative depending on context.
  2217→
  2218→[[axtu_ipv4]]
  2219→{axtu_ipv4}::
  2220→A
  2221→{axl_motif}
  2222→representing an IPv4 address in dotted-decimal notation.
  2223→Format: four octets 0-255 separated by dots (e.g., `192.168.1.1`).
  2224→
  2225→[[axtu_cidr]]
  2226→{axtu_cidr}::
  2227→A
  2228→{axl_motif}
  2229→representing a CIDR block for network addressing.
  2230→Format: IPv4 address followed by slash and prefix length (e.g., `10.0.0.0/8`).
  2231→
  2232→[[axtu_port]]
  2233→{axtu_port}::
  2234→A
  2235→{axl_motif}
  2236→representing a network port number.
  2237→Valid range: 1-65535. Well-known ports are 1-1023.
  2238→
  2239→[[axtu_domain]]
  2240→{axtu_domain}::
  2241→A
  2242→{axl_motif}
  2243→representing a DNS domain name.
  2244→Must start and end with alphanumeric characters; may contain dots and hyphens.
  2245→
  2246→[[axtu_sha256]]
  2247→{axtu_sha256}::
  2248→A
  2249→{axl_motif}
  2250→representing a SHA-256 cryptographic hash.
  2251→Format: 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.
  2252→
  2253→[[axtu_xname]]
  2254→{axtu_xname}::
  2255→A
  2256→{axl_motif}
  2257→representing a cross-context safe identifier.
  2258→Must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens.
  2259→Safe for use in file names, environment variables, and most programming contexts.
  2260→
  2261→== Axial Type Google Terms
  2262→
  2263→{axtg_*} types represent Google Cloud Platform resource identifiers and patterns.
  2264→These types have specific format requirements enforced by GCP APIs.
  2265→
  2266→[[axtg_project_id]]
  2267→{axtg_project_id}::
  2268→A
  2269→{axl_motif}
  2270→representing a Google Cloud Platform project identifier.
  2271→Format: 6-30 lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens; must start with a letter and end with letter or digit.
  2272→Globally unique across all of GCP.
  2273→
  2274→[[axtg_region]]
  2275→{axtg_region}::
  2276→A
  2277→{axl_motif}
  2278→representing a Google Cloud Platform region code.
  2279→Format: lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens (e.g., `us-central1`, `europe-west1`).
  2280→Determines geographic location for regional resources.
  2281→
  2282→[[axtg_service_account]]
  2283→{axtg_service_account}::
  2284→A
  2285→{axl_motif}
  2286→representing a Google Cloud Platform service account email.
  2287→Format: `{account-id}@{project-id}.iam.gserviceaccount.com` where account-id is 6-30 characters.
  2288→Used as identity for programmatic API access.
  2289→
  2290→[[axtg_billing_account]]
  2291→{axtg_billing_account}::
  2292→A
  2293→{axl_motif}
  2294→representing a Google Cloud Platform billing account identifier.
  2295→Format: `XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX` (three groups of six uppercase alphanumeric characters).
  2296→Links projects to payment sources.
  2297→
  2298→[[axtg_project_number]]
  2299→{axtg_project_number}::
  2300→A
  2301→{axl_motif}
  2302→representing a Google Cloud Platform project number.
  2303→Format: numeric identifier automatically assigned at project creation.
  2304→Globally unique across all of GCP.
  2305→Immutable once assigned.
  2306→
  2307→== Axial Infrastructure Google Terms
  2308→
  2309→{axig_*} terms represent Google Cloud Platform infrastructure patterns.
  2310→Each term explicitly declares its level:
  2311→
  2312→* **Service-level**: the managed platform itself
  2313→* **Instance-level**: a particular resource within a service
  2314→* **Operational**: execution patterns and states
  2315→
  2316→=== Service-Level Terms
  2317→
  2318→[[axig_resource_manager]]
  2319→{axig_resource_manager}::
  2320→A
  2321→{axl_motif}
  2322→representing Google Cloud Resource Manager.
  2323→Service-level: the platform for managing organizations, folders, and projects.
  2324→
  2325→[[axig_cloud_storage]]
  2326→{axig_cloud_storage}::
  2327→A
  2328→{axl_motif}
  2329→representing Google Cloud Storage.
  2330→Service-level: the object storage platform, not buckets within it.
  2331→
  2332→[[axig_artifact_registry]]
  2333→{axig_artifact_registry}::
  2334→A
  2335→{axl_motif}
  2336→representing Google Artifact Registry.
  2337→Service-level: the artifact management platform, not repositories within it.
  2338→
  2339→[[axig_cloud_build]]
  2340→{axig_cloud_build}::
  2341→A
  2342→{axl_motif}
  2343→representing Google Cloud Build.
  2344→Service-level: the build execution platform, not individual builds.
  2345→
  2346→[[axig_iam]]
  2347→{axig_iam}::
  2348→A
  2349→{axl_motif}
  2350→representing Google Cloud IAM.
  2351→Service-level: the identity and access management platform.
  2352→
  2353→=== Instance-Level: Resource Manager
  2354→
  2355→[[axig_organization]]
  2356→{axig_organization}::
  2357→A
  2358→{axl_motif}
  2359→representing a Google Cloud organization.
  2360→Instance-level: a particular organization, top of GCP resource hierarchy.
  2361→
  2362→[[axig_folder]]
  2363→{axig_folder}::
  2364→A
  2365→{axl_motif}
  2366→representing a Google Cloud folder.
  2367→Instance-level: an organizational container within an
  2368→{axig_organization}.
  2369→
  2370→[[axig_project]]
  2371→{axig_project}::
  2372→A
  2373→{axl_motif}
  2374→representing a Google Cloud project.
  2375→Instance-level: a resource container with billing, APIs, and IAM.
  2376→
  2377→[[axig_lien]]
  2378→{axig_lien}::
  2379→A
  2380→{axl_motif}
  2381→representing a Google Cloud lien.
  2382→Instance-level: a deletion protection on a resource.
  2383→
  2384→=== Instance-Level: Cloud Storage
  2385→
  2386→[[axig_bucket]]
  2387→{axig_bucket}::
  2388→A
  2389→{axl_motif}
  2390→representing a Cloud Storage bucket.
  2391→Instance-level: an object container with ACLs and lifecycle policies.
  2392→
  2393→=== Instance-Level: Artifact Registry
  2394→
  2395→[[axig_artifact_repository]]
  2396→{axig_artifact_repository}::
  2397→A
  2398→{axl_motif}
  2399→representing an Artifact Registry repository.
  2400→Instance-level: container for container images or generic artifacts.
  2401→
  2402→[[axig_container_image]]
  2403→{axig_container_image}::
  2404→A
  2405→{axl_motif}
  2406→representing a container image in Artifact Registry.
  2407→Instance-level: an image with tags and digest.
  2408→
  2409→[[axig_generic_artifact]]
  2410→{axig_generic_artifact}::
  2411→A
  2412→{axl_motif}
  2413→representing a generic artifact in Artifact Registry.
  2414→Instance-level: a non-container artifact (archive, binary, metadata).
  2415→
  2416→=== Instance-Level: Cloud Build
  2417→
  2418→[[axig_build]]
  2419→{axig_build}::
  2420→A
  2421→{axl_motif}
  2422→representing a Cloud Build execution.
  2423→Instance-level: a particular build processing instructions to create artifacts.
  2424→
  2425→=== Instance-Level: IAM
  2426→
  2427→[[axig_service_account]]
  2428→{axig_service_account}::
  2429→A
  2430→{axl_motif}
  2431→representing a GCP service account.
  2432→Instance-level: a non-human identity for API authentication.
  2433→
  2434→[[axig_role]]
  2435→{axig_role}::
  2436→A
  2437→{axl_motif}
  2438→representing a GCP IAM role.
  2439→Instance-level: a named collection of permissions.
  2440→
  2441→[[axig_binding]]
  2442→{axig_binding}::
  2443→A
  2444→{axl_motif}
  2445→representing a GCP IAM policy binding.
  2446→Instance-level: association of identity to role on a resource scope.
  2447→
  2448→[[axig_service_account_key]]
  2449→{axig_service_account_key}::
  2450→A
  2451→{axl_motif}
  2452→representing a service account key file.
  2453→Instance-level: JSON credential for service account authentication.
  2454→
  2455→=== Operational Terms
  2456→
  2457→[[axig_lro]]
  2458→{axig_lro}::
  2459→A
  2460→{axl_motif}
  2461→representing a Long Running Operation.
  2462→Operational: async pattern returning handle for later completion check.
  2463→
  2464→[[axig_project_state]]
  2465→{axig_project_state}::
  2466→A
  2467→{axl_motif}
  2468→representing a project lifecycle state.
  2469→Operational: conditions like ACTIVE, DELETE_REQUESTED describing status.
  2470→
  2471→== Axial Key Premise Terms
  2472→
  2473→[[axk_premise]]
  2474→{axk_premise}::
  2475→A
  2476→{axl_motif}
  2477→representing a declared design constraint or assumption that explicitly bounds system complexity.
  2478→Premises eliminate classes of problems by stating what the system will not handle, simplifying design.
  2479→
  2480→== Infrastructure Terms
  2481→
  2482→[[axo_dependency]]
  2483→{axo_dependency}::
  2484→A
  2485→{axl_motif}
  2486→representing required component or library for operation.
  2487→Specifies external requirements for functionality.
  2488→
  2489→== Compliance Rules
  2490→
  2491→This section specifies compositional requirements between motifs.
  2492→When a term voices a particular motif, these rules define what companion elements are required.
  2493→
  2494→=== Procedure Documentation Completeness
  2495→
  2496→Voicing as
  2497→{axo_procedure}
  2498→requires documentation sections for:
  2499→
  2500→* {axs_inputs}
  2501→— what the procedure requires to execute
  2502→
  2503→* {axs_behavior}
  2504→— what the procedure does
  2505→
  2506→* {axs_outputs}
  2507→— what the procedure produces
  2508→
  2509→* {axs_completion}
  2510→— how the procedure signals success or failure
  2511→
  2512→Optional sections that MAY be included:
  2513→
  2514→* {axs_preconditions}
  2515→— environmental state requirements before execution
  2516→
  2517→* {axs_postconditions}
  2518→— environmental state guarantees after success
  2519→
  2520→* {axs_errors}
  2521→— failure modes and recovery guidance
  2522→
  2523→=== Command Documentation Completeness
  2524→
  2525→Voicing as
  2526→{axo_command}
  2527→requires all
  2528→{axo_procedure}
  2529→sections plus:
  2530→
  2531→* {axe_environment}
  2532→dimension specifying execution context (e.g.,
  2533→{axe_bash_interactive},
  2534→{axe_bash_unattended})
  2535→
  2536→=== Guide Documentation Completeness
  2537→
  2538→Voicing as
  2539→{axo_guide}
  2540→requires all
  2541→{axo_procedure}
  2542→sections plus:
  2543→
  2544→* {axs_behavior}
  2545→steps use
  2546→{axe_human_guide}
  2547→control voicings
  2548→
  2549→* Steps describe actions the human performs in external systems
  2550→
  2551→=== Lifecycle Dimension Requirement
  2552→
  2553→Every
  2554→{axo_procedure}
  2555→voicing requires exactly one lifecycle dimension:
  2556→
  2557→* {axd_transient}
  2558→— executes and completes (most common)
  2559→
  2560→* {axd_longrunning}
  2561→— runs until stopped
  2562→
  2563→* {axd_periodic}
  2564→— runs on schedule
  2565→
  2566→Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
  2567→
  2568→=== Long-running Procedure Completeness
  2569→
  2570→Voicing with
  2571→{axd_longrunning}
  2572→requires all
  2573→{axo_procedure}
  2574→sections plus:
  2575→
  2576→* {axs_startup}
  2577→— initialization behavior and readiness criteria
  2578→
  2579→* {axs_shutdown}
  2580→— graceful termination and cleanup
  2581→
  2582→Optional sections:
  2583→
  2584→* {axs_health}
  2585→— liveness and readiness probes
  2586→
  2587→* {axs_signals}
  2588→— Unix signal handling
  2589→
  2590→* {axs_recovery}
  2591→— crash recovery behavior
  2592→
  2593→=== Periodic Procedure Completeness
  2594→
  2595→Voicing with
  2596→{axd_periodic}
  2597→requires all
  2598→{axo_procedure}
  2599→sections plus:
  2600→
  2601→* {axs_schedule}
  2602→— timing specification
  2603→
  2604→Optional sections:
  2605→
  2606→* {axs_idempotency}
  2607→— re-run safety guarantees
  2608→
  2609→* {axs_overlap}
  2610→— concurrent execution handling
  2611→
  2612→=== CLI Subcommand Completeness
  2613→
  2614→Voicing as
  2615→{axi_cli_subcommand}
  2616→requires:
  2617→
  2618→* Exit Status section voiced as
  2619→{axa_exit_uniform}
  2620→or
  2621→{axa_exit_enumerated}
  2622→(which voice
  2623→{axs_completion}
  2624→in CLI context)
  2625→
  2626→* Arguments section voiced as
  2627→{axa_argument_list}
  2628→(which voices
  2629→{axs_inputs}
  2630→in CLI context; may list zero arguments if operation takes none)
  2631→
  2632→=== CLI Option Completeness
  2633→
  2634→Voicing as
  2635→{axa_cli_option}
  2636→requires the definition text to specify:
  2637→
  2638→* Long form (e.g., `--file`)
  2639→
  2640→* Short form or explicit absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
  2641→
  2642→* Default value, if the argument is used with
  2643→{axd_defaulted}
  2644→
  2645→=== Argument List Item Qualification
  2646→
  2647→Within an
  2648→{axa_argument_list},
  2649→each item requires a preceding
  2650→{axd_dimension}
  2651→annotation specifying at minimum:
  2652→
  2653→* {axd_required}
  2654→or
  2655→{axd_optional}
  2656→
  2657→* {axd_defaulted}
  2658→if a default value applies
  2659→
  2660→=== Type Voicing Qualification
  2661→
  2662→Voicing a type (subspecializing
  2663→{axt_*}
  2664→or
  2665→{axtu_*}
  2666→motifs) constrains dimension usage.
  2667→
  2668→Valid dimensions:
  2669→
  2670→* {axd_list}
  2671→— type intrinsically represents delimited collection
  2672→
  2673→Invalid dimensions:
  2674→
  2675→* {axd_required},
  2676→{axd_optional},
  2677→{axd_defaulted}
  2678→— presence applies to usage sites, not type definitions
  2679→
  2680→* {axd_repeated}
  2681→— repetition applies to usage sites, not type definitions
  2682→
  2683→=== Regime Specification Completeness
  2684→
  2685→Voicing as
  2686→{axrg_regime}
  2687→requires:
  2688→
  2689→* Format dimension (
  2690→{axf_bash},
  2691→{axf_makefile},
  2692→{axf_json},
  2693→etc.) specifying how assignments are carried
  2694→
  2695→* Provenance dimension (
  2696→{axrd_file_sourced},
  2697→{axrd_env_inherited},
  2698→or
  2699→{axrd_constructed})
  2700→declaring how
  2701→{axrg_assignment_s}
  2702→arrive
  2703→
  2704→=== Regime Variable Qualification
  2705→
  2706→Voicing as
  2707→{axrg_variable}
  2708→requires:
  2709→
  2710→* Type in annotation (base type motif or type voicing)
  2711→
  2712→* At minimum
  2713→{axd_required}
  2714→or
  2715→{axd_optional}
  2716→
  2717→* {axd_defaulted}
  2718→if a default value applies
  2719→
  2720→Valid dimensions:
  2721→
  2722→* {axd_required}
  2723→— variable must have assignment
  2724→
  2725→* {axd_optional}
  2726→— variable may be absent
  2727→
  2728→* {axd_defaulted}
  2729→— fallback value when absent
  2730→
  2731→* {axd_list}
  2732→— inherited from type voicing; variable's type is a delimited collection
  2733→
  2734→Invalid dimensions:
  2735→
  2736→* {axd_repeated}
  2737→— regime files use unique keys; multiple assignments of same variable not permitted
  2738→
  2739→* {axd_nullable}
  2740→— format-dependent;
  2741→{axf_bash}
  2742→has no null distinct from empty string
  2743→
  2744→=== Regime Voicing Annotation Completeness
  2745→
  2746→{axvr_regime}
  2747→requires:
  2748→
  2749→* {axf_format}
  2750→dimension specifying how
  2751→{axrg_assignment_s}
  2752→are carried
  2753→
  2754→{axvr_variable}
  2755→requires:
  2756→
  2757→* Second
  2758→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2759→in definition text must be the parent regime
  2760→
  2761→* {axd_required}
  2762→or
  2763→{axd_optional}
  2764→
  2765→* Type
  2766→{axl_motif}
  2767→(base type or type voicing)
  2768→
  2769→{axvr_group}
  2770→requires:
  2771→
  2772→* Second
  2773→{mcm_attribute_reference}
  2774→in definition text must be the parent regime
  2775→
  2776→=== Regime Hierarchy Consistency
  2777→
  2778→* {axvr_group}
  2779→with
  2780→{axd_conditional}
  2781→requires corresponding
  2782→{axhrgb_group}
  2783→in subdocument to have at least one
  2784→{axhrgc_gate}
  2785→
  2786→* {axvr_group}
  2787→without
  2788→{axd_conditional}:
  2789→corresponding
  2790→{axhrgb_group}
  2791→must have no
  2792→{axhrgc_gate}
  2793→
  2794→* Every
  2795→{axvr_variable}
  2796→under a regime should appear as either
  2797→{axhrv_variable}
  2798→or
  2799→{axhrgv_variable}
  2800→in the subdocument
  2801→
